title: werckleocereus glaber subsp. mirandae
intro: Werckleocereus glaber subsp. mirandae, also known as 'Miranda's Ball Cactus', is a rare and lesser-known species of the Werckleocereus cactus family, native to Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits associated with Werckleocereus glaber subsp. mirandae include relief from pain, wound healing, and potential anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures by drying its stems and leaves to extract its bioactive compounds.
